nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Raineville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Lucien, Armand, Andre, Fernand, Jacques, Alphonse, Antoine, Cecile, Gisele, Jean Guy, Laurent.
French: habitational name from Rainville, a place in the Vosges and in Orne. Compare   Ranville  ,   Raville  , and   Renville  .

History: Paul (de) Rainville from Touques in Calvados, France, married Roline Poette in Touques c. 1638, died in Beauport, QC, in 1686. His descendants also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Ranville, Raville, and Renville.

variants.name: Ranville
meanings: Altered form of French   Rainville  .
History: Bearers of the surname Ranville are descendants of Paul (de) Rainville from France (see Rainville ).

variants.name: Raville
meanings: Altered form of French   Rainville  .
History: Bearers of the surname Raville are descendants of Paul (de) Rainville from France (see Rainville ).

variants.name: Renville
meanings: Altered form of French   Rainville  . This surname is most common among Native Americans in SD and MN, especially among the Sisseton Wahpeton Oyate tribe of Dakota Sioux.

History: The SD and MN Renvilles trace their origin to French Canadian Joseph Rainville or Renville (1753–1806), the father of Joseph Renville, Jr (1779–1846), after whom the town of Renville, MN, is named, and a grandfather (through another son, Victor Renville) of Gabriel Renville, the leader of the Sisseton Wahpeton Oyate tribe of Dakota Sioux in the second half of the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.
